title: quercus faginea f. macrobalanus
intro: Quercus faginea f. macrobalanus, also known as the Portuguese oak, is a subspecies of Quercus faginea, a deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus faginea f. macrobalanus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
